task_completion.description: Agent opens and views the player projections data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a spreadsheet application, text editor, or terminal output displaying the roster of 16 players with their projected stats (yards, touchdowns, receptions, etc.).

task_completion.description: Agent calculates the projected fantasy points for the players using PPR scoring rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a spreadsheet column with formulas, a Python script, or a scratchpad where the agent computes the points (e.g., 1 pt per reception, 0.1 pts per rush/rec yard, 6 pts per rush/rec TD, 0.04 pts per pass yard, 4 pts per pass TD).

task_completion.description: Agent selects a lineup that strictly adheres to the positional constraints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the final selected lineup to ensure it contains exactly 7 players: 1 QB, 2 RBs, 2 WRs, 1 TE, and 1 FLEX (which must be an RB, WR, or TE).

task_completion.description: Agent records the optimal lineup decisions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a saved file, document, or final output explicitly listing the chosen starting lineup. The optimal lineup should yield exactly 148.9 total projected points.

integrity.description: Agent performed actual calculations rather than guessing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of a script, spreadsheet formula, or calculator being used to process the data, rather than the agent just typing out a lineup without showing its work.

integrity.description: Agent recorded the lineup in a file or document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, spreadsheet, or similar application being used to save the final lineup decisions, rather than just leaving the answer in a terminal prompt.

integrity.description: Agent did not use the web to search for fantasy advice.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not open a web browser to search for fantasy football rankings or optimal lineups, as the task uses synthetic local data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses standard PPR scoring: 1 point per reception, 0.1 points per rushing/receiving yard, 6 points per rushing/receiving TD, 0.04 points per passing yard, and 4 points per passing TD. The valid roster must have exactly 7 players: 1 QB, 2 RB, 2 WR, 1 TE, and 1 FLEX (RB, WR, or TE). The optimal lineup based on the provided synthetic data yields exactly 148.9 total points.
